draft.introduction: Micah’s summary of prophetic religion in a single verse: do justice, love mercy, walk humbly with God. Often called the greatest one-verse distillation of the Hebrew prophetic tradition.

translation.text: With what shall I come before the Lord, and bow myself before God on high? Shall I come before him with burnt offerings, with calves a year old? Will the Lord be pleased with thousands of rams? He has told you, O man, what is good; and what does the Lord require of you but to do justice, and to love kindness, and to walk humbly with your God?

translator_notes.note: “Love kindness” translates Hebrew hesed, the covenantal love-loyalty that is God’s defining attribute. Micah demands not merely that Israel practice mercy but that they desire it—an inward disposition, not merely outward compliance.
